svg.text: src/gemm.c · which cells of A, B, C one output tile (row i, column tile j:j+vl) touches, for one unrolled l..l+7 step
one float at a time — not vector-accelerated
same l-step, different j (other tiles read it)
vectorizes across l, only across j:j+vl
teal = full rows l..l+7; only j:j+vl feeds this tile

Amber = RVV vector instructions (B’s vle64_v_f64m1 loads, C’s vector load/store) — vl elements moved per instruction. Blue = plain scalar float loads (A) — one element per instruction, never vectorized here.
Numbered arrows: each scalar aN pairs with row l+N of B (blue, 1-to-1) — those 8 vector products then all converge into the same C[i, j:j+vl] (amber funnel, the reduction).
Repeating this over all l (rightward through B/A) accumulates the full row; repeating over all j (rightward through C) and i (downward through A/C) covers the whole output.
see opt_gemm_dataflow.svg for the register/instruction-level view of this same tile
